time.localHint: Local clock at the event / place GPS
time.userHint: Your clock per your profile timezone

visibility.label: Visibility
visibility.allowedEmailsHint: Emails separated by newline, comma, or whitespace. Users must have an Astrozor account — unknown emails are skipped.
visibility.memberGate: This content is for selected users only

picker.grantedEmpty: Nobody yet. Drag a user here or double-click.
picker.hint: Double-click or drag-and-drop to move users between lists. The owner always has access and doesn't appear in the lists.

composer.previewEmpty: Preview is empty — markdown didn't render.
composer.nothingToPreview: Type something in the Write tab first.
composer.heading: Heading

profile.title: User profile
profile.empty: User hasn't filled in any information yet.

actions.confirmDelete: Really delete "{{name}}"? This cannot be undone.

form.name: Name
form.kindHint: Only admins can create permanent observatories/spots. Your place will be temporary (expires after 4 h unless you set valid_to).
form.openingHoursHelp: Up to 2 intervals per day (e.g. 8:00–12:00 and 13:00–17:00). With 'Auto check-in' ticked, the system automatically posts an anonymous 'Hvězdárna otevřena' presence during those hours.
form.openingHoursNotePlaceholder: E.g. 'By appointment', reservation link, seasonal restrictions…
form.autoCheckinHint: The map will show an anonymous 'open' presence during these hours.

chat.empty: No messages yet. Be the first.
chat.loginToViewHint: Sign in or create an account to see the conversation between observers about this site.
chat.youtubeInvalid: That doesn't look like a YouTube URL.

bortle.title: Bortle dark-sky scale
bortle.subtitle: 9-step rating of night-sky brightness (1 = pristine dark, 9 = inner city)

rows.desc: Milky Way casts shadows, zodiacal light all night, M33 naked-eye
Detailed Milky Way; faint galaxies in binoculars
Milky Way well structured; bright zodiacal light
Milky Way visible but not detailed; light domes over towns
Milky Way only at zenith, washed out; M31 just a smudge
Milky Way barely visible; sky greyish, nebulae only in scope
Orange-grey sky; Milky Way gone, only bright stars
Only bright stars and planets; whole sky pale
Just Moon, planets and a few of the brightest stars

mastoRail.title: Community on Mastodon
mastoRail.opensOnMastodon: Opens on Mastodon in a new tab
mastoRail.homeRequiresAuth: Sign in and connect a Mastodon account in Settings
mastoRail.author: Author
mastoRail.hashtagHint: Separate multiple hashtags by comma or space (e.g. nasa, astronomy). The # is added for you. Matches any of them (OR).

articles.calloutTitle: How to publish Quarto articles from VS Code or RStudio
articles.calloutBody: Publish full Quarto and R Markdown documents without copying the text — straight from the editor you already use. Step-by-step guide for the astrozorpub RStudio addin and the VS Code path.

publishIntro.title: Astrozor accepts articles from every tool you use
publishIntro.body: Markdown, Quarto, RMarkdown, Jupyter — every path leads to the same article with DOI, comments, and sharing. Writing in VS Code, RStudio, or Jupyter? We ship an addin / extension that renders and uploads for you.
publishIntro.bullet1: 🧮 Interactive charts (plotly, leaflet, htmlwidgets) stay live in the article iframe — readers can hover and zoom
publishIntro.bullet2: 📐 LaTeX equations via MathJax, syntax-highlighted code blocks, code-fold for long snippets
publishIntro.bullet3: 🔁 Idempotent publish — same slug = update of the existing article, no duplicates, DOI preserved

astrozor.title: Astrozor in-app editor
astrozor.desc: Markdown editor with live preview. Nothing to install, just a browser.

vscode.title: VS Code extension
vscode.desc: Publishes .md, .qmd, and HTML folders. Token in Secret Storage.

rstudio.title: RStudio addin
rstudio.desc: astrozorpub package — render + publish .qmd / .Rmd in one click.

jupyter.title: Jupyter notebook
jupyter.desc: nbconvert + VS Code Publish folder, or curl with a ZIP.

articles.commentsEmpty: No comments yet. Be the first.
articles.commentsDraftHint: Comments are available once the article is published.
articles.emptyHint: Click 'New article' to write the first one.

intro.title: 📘 How to publish — supported formats & RStudio addin
intro.markdownDesc: Markdown — write directly in the app via New article. Editor with preview, HTML sanitization, embedded videos, LaTeX math.
intro.quartoDesc: Quarto (.qmd) — render locally in RStudio (with plotly interactive graphs, OJS, R/Python computations) and publish via the RStudio addin or manually Import.
intro.rmarkdownDesc: R Markdown (.Rmd) — same flow as Quarto, just rendered via rmarkdown::render().
intro.jupyterDesc: Jupyter (.ipynb) — render with jupyter nbconvert --to html and upload the ZIP via Import. Direct support via a VS Code extension is on the roadmap.
intro.installIntro: After installation you'll find Publish to Astrozor in the Addins menu. Set the token once via Settings → API tokens.
intro.vscodeNote: VS Code extension — planned (Q3 2026). Will share the same API as the RStudio addin: local render, ZIP, upload to this instance.

import.intro: Upload a ZIP containing rendered HTML (index.html + assets). For the automated flow from RStudio use the astrozorpub addin.
import.title: Article title

editor.doiHint: On publish a DOI will be issued on your connected Zenodo account.
editor.doiUnavailable: To mint a DOI, first add a Zenodo token in Settings → Integrations.
editor.preRenderedTitle: Article content is managed by an external renderer
editor.preRenderedBody: This is a pre-rendered {{engine}} bundle — the HTML was rendered locally and is served via iframe. Only the title and short description are editable. To change the content, re-publish from RStudio / VS Code (same slug overwrites the bundle), or upload a new ZIP via Import (Quarto/RMd).
editor.coverDropHere: Drop an image or click to pick
editor.coverHint: JPEG / PNG / WebP / GIF · server resizes to 1600 px

auth.welcome: Welcome to Astrozor. Please log in or sign up to continue.

oauth.notConfigured: {{provider}} sign-in isn't configured by the administrator yet.
oauth.errorFlash: Sign-in failed: {{error}}. Try again or choose another method.

errors.identity_owned_by_another_user: This account is already linked to a different Astrozor user. If it's yours, sign in there and add the SSO from that account — or unlink it there first.
errors.bad_state: Sign-in session expired mid-flow. Please try again.
errors.no_code: The provider didn't return an authentication code. Try again or use another method.
errors.not_configured: This provider isn't configured on this Astrozor install yet.
errors.identity_email_not_verified: Your email at the provider isn't verified. Verify it in the provider's settings, then try again.

oauth.notConfiguredHint: Not configured on this instance — admin must add OAuth credentials to .env
oauth.errorBadState: Invalid OAuth state — please try again.
oauth.errorNotConfigured: GitHub OAuth is not configured on this instance.
oauth.errorIdentityOwned: This account is already linked to another user.

magicLink.intro: We'll send you a link that signs you in. No password needed.
magicLink.sent: ✓ If the email is registered, a link was sent.
magicLink.devHint: In dev, open {{url}} (MailHog) and click the link.

signup.mailhogHint: Verification e-mail arrives in MailHog at {{url}}.

docs.fallbackBanner: This page isn't translated yet — showing the English version.

admin.title: Application admin
admin.subtitle: Self-hosted map infrastructure + user management.
admin.notStaff: This section is only available to application admins.

pmtiles.title: PMTiles (map tiles)
pmtiles.refreshHint: Restarts the download. With the same URL it resumes via HTTP Range (no-op). Change the URL to a newer Protomaps build to fetch a fresh ~130 GB.
pmtiles.backToOsmHint: Disable self-hosted PMTiles and route the map back to public OSM tile servers (online, rate-limited).
pmtiles.deleteConfirm: This removes the downloaded PMTiles archive ({{size}}). The map will fall back to public OSM tiles. Continue?

photon.title: Photon (search backend)
photon.backToNominatimHint: Disable self-hosted Photon and route place search back to public Nominatim (online, rate-limited).
photon.deployHint: Run `docker compose --profile photon up -d photon`, import OSM extract per docs/runbook/map-infra.md, then click 'Probe availability'.
photon.resetConfirm: Wipes the downloaded Photon data, restarts the container, kicks off a fresh import (~hours). Continue?

places.title: Places
places.subtitle: All places in the application ({{count}}). Edit, delete, or bulk-import from CSV.
places.confirmDelete: Really delete "{{name}}"? Bortle measurement history and check-ins will be removed too.

import.title: Import places from CSV
import.hint: CSV with header: name, kind, lat, lon (+ optional description, address, website, contact, opening_hours, elevation_m, bortle_manual, owner_email). Rows within 200 m of an existing place are flagged as duplicates.

chat.title: Place-detail chat
chat.subtitle: Rules for new messages in the per-place discussion threads.
chat.saved: Saved. Applies to new messages. Older messages are not trimmed.
chat.outOfRange: Value must be between 200 and 50 000 characters.
chat.hint: Limit covers both rich-text HTML markup (e.g. <strong>) and visible text. A new value takes effect immediately, but only on newly posted messages — existing rows are untouched.

lp.title: Light pollution map
lp.subtitle: Data source for the overlay layer and the automatic Bortle estimate on new places.
lp.notCached: No local data (overlay fetched from NASA on every view)
lp.deleteConfirm: This removes the local LP tile cache ({{mib}} MiB) for this source. Continue?

black_marble_2016.title: NASA Black Marble 2016 (static)
black_marble_2016.description: Annual snapshot 'Earth at Night 2016' — single composite, never changes.
black_marble_2016.pros: Consistent, always available, cached tile never changes; zero maintenance.
black_marble_2016.cons: 10 years old, does not reflect LED retrofitting from 2018–2024.

viirs_dnb_latest.title: VIIRS DNB latest (daily)
viirs_dnb_latest.description: VIIRS SNPP Day/Night Band radiance, daily composite from the latest satellite pass. Manual refresh required.
viirs_dnb_latest.pros: Up-to-date data (usually 2-3 days lag), captures recent changes in light intensity.
viirs_dnb_latest.cons: Variable: moon phase, clouds and snow can distort values. Requires periodic Refresh.

projects.title: Projects

intro.title: Build the astronomy network with us
intro.body: Astrozor connects people around concrete tasks — from observation campaigns to citizen science and tooling. Real work is tracked as GitHub issues attached to each project. Pick something that interests you and jump in.
intro.bullet1: Find a project you like and open its issues list.
intro.bullet2: Use the "🙋 Take this on" button to claim a task.
intro.bullet3: The leaderboard below shows who is working on what.

leaderboard.title: Top solvers
leaderboard.empty: No one is currently assigned to open issues.
leaderboard.countTooltip: Number of open issues this user is solving

projects.creatorCantLeave: The owner can't leave their own project — transfer ownership in a future release.
projects.emptyHint: Click 'New project' — track GitHub repos and collaborators.

status.notFoundHint: GitHub returned 404 — either this repository doesn't exist, or it's private and your connected GitHub account doesn't have it in scope. For private repos, disconnect and reconnect GitHub in Settings (we ask for the 'repo' scope). Then click Refresh.
status.rateLimitedHint: Anonymous quota is 60 requests/hour. Connect GitHub in Settings to raise it to 5000/hour.

issues.title: Open tasks (issues)
issues.togglePRTooltip_one: This repo also has {{count}} open pull request (not shown in the list)
issues.togglePRTooltip_other: This repo also has {{count}} open pull requests (not shown in the list)
issues.repoUnreachable: Cannot load issues — repository is not visible to your connected GitHub account (see the warning above).
issues.claimNotCollaborator: GitHub didn't assign you — you're not a collaborator on this repo. Ask the project owner to add you as a collaborator.
issues.claimNoToken: Connect your GitHub account in Settings to claim issues.
issues.needGhConnect: Connect your GitHub account in Settings to claim issues.
issues.chatEmpty: No messages in this thread yet.
issues.chatPlaceholder: Write a comment about this issue…
issues.postsToGh: Posts as a GitHub comment under your linked GH account
issues.newIssueTitlePlaceholder: Briefly describe the problem or request
issues.newIssueBodyPlaceholder: What happened, how to reproduce, or why the request matters…
issues.newIssuePostsToGh: Posts to GitHub under your linked GH account
issues.newIssueNoToken: No connected GitHub account in Settings — link it via OAuth to file tickets.

activity.heading: Commit activity (365 days)
activity.empty: No commits in the last 365 days across all GH repos of this project.

events.title: Events
events.includeSprintsHint: Sprints from Zooniverse projects mixed into the list

calendar.campaignsOnDay: Citizen Science campaigns on this day

events.terminalState: Terminal state — event won't transition further.
events.confirmDelete: Really delete event "{{title}}"? Registrations and discussion will also be removed.
events.discussionEmpty: No messages yet. Be the first.

googleCalendar.tooltip: Opens Google Calendar with the event pre-filled — just click Save.
googleCalendar.loginFirst: Sign in first to add events to your calendar.
googleCalendar.connectFirst: Link your Google account in Settings so the event lands in your calendar.

events.registrationNotOpen: Registration is not open right now.

field.title: Title
field.meetingGenerateHint: Creates a random Jitsi room and fills the URL
field.discordGenHint: Creates a channel + invite in your connected Discord server using the Astrozor bot
field.discordGenSaveFirst: Save the event first to enable Discord channel generation
field.discordGenConnectFirst: Connect Discord in Settings (Connected accounts → Discord) first
field.discordHowto: How to get an invite link?
field.discordHowtoSteps:
• Open your Discord server and pick the channel for the event
  • Right-click the channel → Invite People
  • Edit invite link → set Expire after: Never and Max uses: No limit (or as needed)
  • Click Copy and paste the URL like https://discord.gg/XYZ here
field.geocacheHint: Cache code (e.g. GC1ABCDE) or full geocaching.com URL
field.localTimeHint: Enter times as LOCAL CLOCK at the event location — i.e. the time attendees on site will see on their watches. Astrozor converts to UTC and shows everyone the value in their own timezone.

location.mapHint: Click on the map to set the venue

citizen.subtitle: Support research by classifying images directly from Astrozor. Pick a project, join the Astrozor group on Zooniverse, classify.
citizen.emptyProjects: No projects in the catalogue yet.
citizen.emptyProjectsHint: An admin will add Zooniverse projects in the admin section.
citizen.noActiveWorkflows: This project has no active classification workflows.

workflowActivity.tooltip: You've already classified in this workflow ({{count}}×). Continue on Zooniverse.

talk.heading: Zooniverse Talk
talk.subtitle: Official Zooniverse discussion boards for this project. Browse threads and comments. To post, sign in on Zooniverse.

citizen.classifyAnonHint: Sign in and link your Zooniverse account so classifications count toward our group stats.
citizen.activeCampaignsHint: Time-boxed Astrozor community sprints — often built around a project from the grid above.
citizen.campaignsForProjectEmpty: No active Astrozor campaign for this project yet.

sprints.heading: Astrozor sprints
sprints.subtitle: Group sprints — pick one workflow, set a window, classify together. After the sprint closes, you see stats just for that window.
sprints.empty: No sprints yet. Open the first one above.
sprints.closeConfirm: Close sprint "{{title}}"? The end date will be set to now and status switched to closed.
sprints.joinToChat: Join the sprint with the button above to enter the discussion. If this isn't your sprint, you can go back.
sprints.joinPromptBody: Joining unlocks the group chat, stats, and activity tracking for the sprint window. You can leave anytime — your contributions stay.

chat.heading: Sprint discussion
chat.empty: No messages yet. Start the conversation.
chat.placeholder: Write a message to sprint members…
chat.attachDialogHint: Paste a numeric subject ID or a classifier / Talk URL. The backend fetches a preview.
chat.attachResolveFailed: Subject not found. Check the ID or URL.
chat.recentEmpty: You haven't classified anything in this project yet.
chat.reconnectBody:
Your Zooniverse account is linked in Astrozor but its OAuth tokens are missing (likely linked before a token-storage update). To fix:
1. Open Account → Connected identities
2. Disconnect "Zooniverse"
3. Reconnect Zooniverse — a fresh OAuth runs and stores the tokens.
Favorites, Collections and Recently classified will start populating after that.
chat.favoritesEmpty: No favorite subjects yet. Click some ❤ on Zooniverse and come back.
chat.collectionsEmpty: You don't have any collections. Create them on Zooniverse.

form.title: New sprint
form.noActiveWorkflowsHint: Project has no active workflows — sprint will be created but classification won't work until Zooniverse re-enables a workflow.

stats.heading: Sprint stats
stats.windowOpen: Sprint is open — counting from {{from}} to today ({{to}}).
stats.noStart: No start date set — stats cannot be computed without a window.
stats.topContributorsEmpty: No classifications in this sprint window yet.
stats.scopeNote: Stats cover all Astrozor group members who classified this Zooniverse project in the window above. Sprint participants are only users who clicked Join.

zombieWarning.title: This project isn't currently running
zombieWarning.body: The project still exists on Zooniverse, but it has never been officially launch-approved and its subject sets are empty. The classify page will open blank — there are no images to classify. Pick a different project from the catalogue.

citizen.notLaunchApproved: Not officially launched on Zooniverse — likely inactive.

disconnect.title: Disconnect project from Astrozor
disconnect.subtitle: Removes the project from the Astrozor catalogue and deletes all our records about it. Nothing is touched on Zooniverse — their project, classifications, and group membership stay intact.
disconnect.consequencesHeading: What will be deleted in Astrozor

willDelete.participants_one: {{count}} Astrozor sprint participant (opt-in record only, not the Zooniverse account)

disconnect.noDownstreamHint: No sprints, stats or participants are attached yet. Disconnect is safe.
disconnect.zooniverseNoteList:
• The Zooniverse project keeps running normally
  • Your personal classifications stay on Zooniverse
  • Astrozor group members stay in the Zooniverse group
  • You can re-add the project from admin any time
disconnect.irreversibleWarning: Deleted sprints (including participants) cannot be restored. The project record itself can be re-created from Panoptes.

result.success: Project disconnected. Deleted: {{sprints}} sprints, {{participants}} participants, {{snapshots}} snapshots.

importReview.title: Confirm project import
importReview.subtitle: Review the status below before adding to the catalogue.
importReview.alreadyInCatalogue: This project is already in the Astrozor catalogue.
importReview.noActiveWorkflows: No active workflows — classification won't work.
importReview.zombieWarning: This project looks inactive — state is "live" but it's not launch-approved and every workflow has empty subject sets. The classify page will open blank — no images to classify.

stats.topContributorsEmpty: Top contributors will appear once someone in the group classifies.

join.anonBody: Sign in and link your Zooniverse account so your classifications count toward the group stats.
join.linkBody: Once linked we add you to the Astrozor group with one click.
join.memberTitle: You are a member of Astrozor
join.memberHint: your classifications count toward group stats
join.joinTitle: Join the Astrozor group on Zooniverse
join.joinBody: Zooniverse opens, confirm the join, then come back. Membership is verified automatically.

campaigns.title: Citizen Science Campaigns
campaigns.emptyHint: Create a campaign to coordinate measurements with contributors.

field.title: Title
field.zooniverseProjectHint: Optional — when linked, the campaign shows on the Zooniverse project detail and on the events calendar.

email.devHint: In dev, all verification e-mails are captured by MailHog (http://localhost:8025).

profile.title: Profile

location.searchTooltip: Geocode the typed label and fill in coordinates
location.detectTooltip: Use the browser's GPS / IP-based position
location.noCoords: No coordinates set — region mode shares the label only
location.errorNoResults: No results for that label. Try a different wording.
location.errorPermission: The browser denied permission to read your location.
location.hintPrecise: Shares precise GPS coordinates + label (shown on the map and profile).
location.hintRegion: Shares only the text label (e.g. "Prague"); coordinates stay private.
location.hintHidden: Doesn't share your location at all.

profile.timezoneHint: Used to render your local time next to events and articles. The UTC value in the DB stays the same.

integrations.title: External integrations (per-user)
integrations.discordHint: Paste a Discord webhook URL — notifications go here. Create in your Discord server: Channel Settings → Integrations → Webhooks.
integrations.zenodoStored: (stored — paste a new value to change)
integrations.zenodoPlaceholder: Paste token — we never display it back
integrations.zenodoHint: When you publish an article, the DOI is registered on your Zenodo account. Generate a token at
integrations.autopostCheckinHint: When you check in at a place, Astrozor posts a "🔭 Observing from …" status to your Mastodon instance. Requires a connected Mastodon account. Anonymous check-ins are never posted.

discord.title: Discord notifications — event types
discord.subtitle: Pick which events should be pinged to your Discord webhook (URL above).
discord.noWebhook: Set a Discord webhook URL above first — without it there's nowhere to send notifications.

kindHint.place_followed_checkin: Someone checked in at a place you're following.
kindHint.place_any_checkin: Anyone, anywhere. Can be high volume.
kindHint.event_status_changed: Optionally restrict to organizers, specific events, and/or target states.
kindHint.project_lifecycle: Restrict to creation or archival only.
kindHint.campaign_status_changed: Optionally restrict to coordinators, specific campaigns, and/or target states.

tokens.title: API tokens
tokens.subtitle: For the RStudio addin, VS Code extension, or command line. The token is shown ONLY ONCE at creation — copy it now.
tokens.envHint: Add it to ~/.Renviron as ASTROZOR_TOKEN=ast_… and restart the R session.

mastodon.title: Mastodon feed
mastodon.noByAuthor: This author hasn't posted in the current batch.

share.title: Share article on Mastodon
share.preview: Toot preview — what your followers will see
share.hashtagsHint: Separate with spaces or commas; the # is added for you.

ctl.title: Map controls
ctl.cloudsOsmHint: 💡 Tip: clouds show best on Dark or Satellite map style.

lightPollutionHint.black_marble_2016: NASA Black Marble (VIIRS DNB 2016 baseline). Yellow/white = heavily lit night skies.
lightPollutionHint.viirs_dnb_latest: NASA VIIRS DNB latest ({{date}}). Live nightly image — yellow/white = heavily lit skies.
